title: Which Leonardo DiCaprio Role Are You, Actually?
dek: Eight iconic characters, zero right answers — let's find out if you're a romantic idealist, a reckless wolf, or a full-on unraveling mind.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers — just go with your gut and let your inner Leo reveal himself.

questions.question: It's Friday night. What's the vibe?

answers.text: Sneak into first class, wing it completely
Whatever's loudest, most expensive, and involves a toast
Holding court, impeccably dressed, making everyone uncomfortable
A tightly planned evening with a backup plan for the backup plan

questions.question: Pick an outfit that defines you.

answers.text: Something loud, tailored, and obscenely expensive
White suit, cigar, a smile that spells trouble
A sharp grey suit — nothing flashy, everything intentional
Immaculate, monogrammed, and quietly out of date
My own cruelty, dressed up as charm
I can't let go of the past
My own impossibly high standards
Grief I refuse to put down
Controlling dreams. Literally.
Seeing details nobody else can
Surviving absolutely anything thrown at me
Solving any mystery — even the ones about myself

questions.question: What would your enemies say about you?

answers.text: A genius who's completely unraveling
Impossible to kill. Unfortunately.
Not who he says he is
Somehow always one step ahead

questions.question: How do you handle a full-blown crisis?

answers.text: Grit my teeth and physically push through it
Convince myself a different story is true
Talk, forge, or charm my way out
Make a grand romantic gesture and hope for the best
A grief I simply cannot let go of
Sweeping someone off their feet with a great story
Grand, impulsive romantic gestures
Extravagant gifts, no budget in sight
Redemption, somehow, after all the lies
Bittersweet, tragic, unforgettable
Chaotic — and somehow I still land on my feet
A twist where the villain finally gets what's coming

questions.question: Choose a setting for your biopic.

answers.text: A doomed ocean liner
A trading floor gone completely off the rails
A plantation mansion dripping with menace
A dream, inside a dream, inside a dream

questions.question: Pick the one word people always use to describe you.

answers.text: Excessive
Menacing
Controlled
Obsessive

results.name: Jack Dawson
results.subtitle: Titanic (1997)
results.description: You're all heart — impulsive, romantic, and completely unwilling to play it safe when it actually matters. You'd sketch a stranger, win a ticket in a poker game, and fall in love before the ship even leaves port, consequences be damned.

results.name: Jordan Belfort
results.subtitle: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013)
results.description: You live loud, chase excess, and genuinely don't know when to stop — for better and for very obviously worse. This role got DiCaprio a Best Actor nomination for a reason: you talk your way into anything, and everyone in the room somehow ends up buying it.

results.name: Calvin Candie
results.subtitle: Django Unchained (2012)
results.description: You're charming right up until the exact moment you're chilling, and honestly that's the whole appeal. Underneath the hospitality and the cigar smoke is someone who enjoys the power a little too much.

results.name: Dom Cobb
results.subtitle: Inception (2010)
results.description: You're the strategist — always thinking three layers deep, always in control, even when your own past is the thing you can't quite navigate. You'd rather build an elaborate plan than admit what you're actually running from.

results.name: Howard Hughes
results.subtitle: The Aviator (2004)
results.description: You're a genius and a perfectionist who can't quite turn the noise in your head off. The film portrays Hughes' life as he grows more unstable due to severe obsessive-compulsive disorder even while achieving greatness — and honestly, that tension is very you.

results.name: Hugh Glass
results.subtitle: The Revenant (2015)
results.description: You are, quite simply, built different. This is the role that finally earned DiCaprio his first Oscar after five nominations, and it's not hard to see why — you'd crawl out of your own grave just to finish what you started.

results.name: Teddy Daniels
results.subtitle: Shutter Island (2010)
results.description: Nothing about your reality is as stable as it looks, and deep down you kind of know it. DiCaprio called this one of the hardest roles of his career, and your mind runs on the same fog-covered logic as his — always one twist away from unraveling completely.

results.name: Frank Abagnale Jr.
results.subtitle: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
results.description: You could talk your way into a cockpit, a hospital, or a courtroom, and somehow people would just let you. The real Frank Abagnale allegedly cashed $2.5 million worth of bad checks while posing as a pilot, a doctor, and a lawyer — and you've got that same slippery, can't-catch-me energy.
